description: Lock Town Wall Maintenance Taxes to York-style murage/tallage, income rights, and wall-maintenance records rather than municipal debt bonds or castle-garrison logistics.

checks.reason: The node concerns taxes and duties for maintaining a fortified urban wall circuit.
Murage, tallage, and related charges are tax mechanisms rather than debt instruments.
Taxation is the shared fiscal context for this local wall-maintenance finance, not a single hard technical prerequisite.
The source-backed scope includes records, orders, income rights, and leased maintenance income.
Tax accounting is contextual administrative infrastructure for wall-maintenance finance.
Municipal debt bonds should not be restored unless the node is rescoped to bond-funded wall finance.
Castle garrison logistics should not carry the municipal wall-maintenance tax scope.
The node remains anchored to 14th-century York records of wall maintenance finance.
